N1.label: Australian Compounder
N1.description: Comps stabilise above 4%; drive-thru pipeline converts at 23+ openings/year; market re-rates back toward growth multiple
N1.plain_english: The market is conflating US failure, escrow supply, and cyclical deceleration with core domestic business health. Australian unit economics are widening (drive-thru AUV A$6.9M, 22% margins, 48-53% franchisee ROI). If comps re-accelerate, GYG can compound at 15-20% revenue growth for the next three to five years.
N1.what_to_watch: H1 FY26 Australian comparable sales. Above 6% confirms cyclical deceleration; below 3% rules this out.
N1.upside: Full compounder re-rating toward A$26.34 (20-30x FY27 EBITDA). Requires comps above 6% and positive filing-day reaction.
N1.risk_plain: Three consecutive negative filing-day reactions and comps decelerating from 15% to 4%. If McDonald's continues to outperform on comps, the premium positioning erodes.

N2.label: Decelerating Growth
N2.description: Growth normalising from hyper-growth IPO phase; IPO multiple was scarcity-driven mispricing; re-rates to 15x EBITDA
N2.plain_english: Comparable sales continue decelerating toward 2-3%. McDonald's and KFC adapt toward cleaner ingredients. The market consensus settles on a mature franchise operator at 15x EBITDA rather than the 90x peak. US losses persist as a consolidated drag. The IPO premium unwinds completely.
N2.what_to_watch: Comps below 3% confirms structural deceleration. McDonald's outperforming GYG on comps for three consecutive quarters confirms competitive convergence.
N2.risk_plain: This is the modal outcome at 46% probability. Implies further downside to A$11.77 from current A$17.53.

N3.label: US Capital Destruction
N3.description: US expansion actively destroys value; governance cannot force course correction; impairment risk 70-140% of NPAT
N3.plain_english: The US expansion is actively destroying capital. EBITDA losses of A$13.2M in FY25 are doubling annually. The parent entity has already written off its entire A$28.2M US investment. Insider control (44%) prevents the board from forcing a course correction on US capital allocation.
N3.what_to_watch: US EBITDA loss trajectory in H1 FY26. If it exceeds A$8M (annualising above A$16M), N3 gains 5-8pp.
N3.risk_plain: US restaurant-level margins are negative 57.5%. The 15-restaurant cap is management-imposed and non-binding.

N4.label: Franchise System Stress
N4.description: Headline franchise metrics mask emerging system stress; franchise flywheel runs in reverse
N4.plain_english: Franchisees on royalty relief have increased from 4 to 6. If median franchise ROI declines below 40% or relief escalates beyond 10 stores, the system deteriorates. The franchise flywheel runs in reverse: lower ROI leads to fewer repeat franchisees, slower openings, and lower network sales.
N4.what_to_watch: Royalty relief count (currently 6), median franchise ROI (currently 48-53%), and reacquisition pace.
N4.risk_plain: Tail risk at 5%. Counter-evidence is strong (ROI 48-53%, 64% repeat rate). N4 exists for monitoring, not as a central scenario.

big_picture: Guzman y Gomez is an Australian fast-casual QSR chain with 256 restaurants and A$468M revenue (FY25). Unit economics are strong (22% restaurant-level margins, 48-53% franchisee ROI, A$6.9M drive-thru AUV) but comparable sales have decelerated from 15% (FY23) to 4-5% (H1 FY26). US operations are loss-making (A$13.2M EBITDA loss, losses doubling annually). The evidence-weighted price is A$13.59, approximately 23% below the market price. H1 FY26 results are the discriminating event.

trend.structure: Persistent lower highs; punctuated by a sharp gap-down in September 2025

support.method: Psychological round number and current 12-month low zone

priorSpikes.context: Gap-down from A$29 to A$23.70; likely earnings shock

inflectionPoints.event: Death cross forms; 50-day MA crosses below 200-day MA
Sharp gap-down; falls 18% in one week
New 12-month low; at 55% below IPO peak
